_meta.description: 70B-nominal-parameter LLM trained from scratch at 0.1 BPW using LittleBit-inspired latent factorization. Binary weight matrices (±1) via dual-path SVD factorization with multi-scale FP16 compensation. Fits in ~2.1 GB RAM at inference. Designed to outperform models 10x its physical size.
novelty_claims: First architecture to natively train 70B-class LLM from scratch at 0.1 BPW without pre-trained teacher
Progressive Binarization Schedule (PBS) — anneals from BF16 to binary over first 5% of training tokens
Dual-Path Orthogonal Init (DPOI) — replaces Dual-SVID for from-scratch initialization
Adaptive STE-v2 — gradient estimator with layer-wise dynamic clipping
Inherent KV-cache compression of ~20x via latent projection caching

model_architecture._comment: Core Transformer dimensions — 70B nominal params at ~0.1 BPW fits in ~2.1 GB
model_architecture.flops_per_token_inference: ~14 GFLOP equivalent (binary ops mapped to XNOR+popcount)

quantization._comment: LittleBit-style latent binarization. W ≅ diag(h) · U_sign · diag(l) · V_sign^T · diag(g)
quantization.paper_reference: LittleBit: Ultra-Low-Bit Quantization via Latent Factorization (NeurIPS 2025)

latent_factorization._comment: W ∈ R^{d_out × d_in} → U_sign ∈ {±1}^{d_out × r}, V_sign ∈ {±1}^{d_in × r}
latent_factorization._rank_derivation: r = d × BPW_per_path / 2 = 8192 × 0.05 / 2 ≈ 205 per path → total effective rank 410

scale_factors._comment: Three learnable FP16 scales compensate for binary information loss
scale_factors._explanation: Scale LR is 5x higher than base LR — scales do the heavy lifting in from-scratch training

residual_compensation._explanation: Primary path initialized with Kaiming-orthogonal binary U,V. Residual path initialized to compensate reconstruction error of primary path at step 0.

layers_quantized._embeddings_note: Embeddings kept BF16 — vocab lookup is not a matmul bottleneck, precision loss here is catastrophic
layers_quantized._lm_head_note: LM head kept BF16 — final logit precision critical for sampling quality

effective_memory_breakdown._note: Embeddings dominate. Consider 4-bit embedding quantization for edge deployment to cut to ~1.2 GB total.

attention._comment: Grouped Query Attention + FlashAttention-3 + RoPE YaRN for 128K context

rope_scaling._note: YaRN extends 8K → 128K context with minimal perplexity degradation

kv_cache_optimization._comment: LittleBit inherent KV compression — cache latent states not full hidden
kv_cache_optimization._cache_dim_note: Store V·x ∈ R^r instead of full K,V ∈ R^{d_model} — ~40x KV cache reduction

sliding_window_attention._note: Alternating global/local attention pattern (Mistral-style) for long-context efficiency

mixture_of_experts._comment: MoE every 4th layer for +capacity at same compute. 8 experts, top-2 routing.
mixture_of_experts._expert_capacity_note: Expert FFN is 0.25× full FFN size — 2 active experts = 0.5× dense FFN compute
mixture_of_experts._z_loss_note: Z-loss prevents router collapse — critical for stable from-scratch MoE training

normalization._note: RMSNorm in FP32 even during BF16 training — prevents norm explosion with binary weights

initialization._comment: DPOI = Dual-Path Orthogonal Init — replaces Dual-SVID when no pretrained weights exist
initialization.description: Initialize U, V factors using semi-orthogonal random matrices (Gram-Schmidt on Gaussian), then take sign(). Scale factors initialized to preserve expected activation magnitude.

residual_path_init.description: Sample U_res, V_res orthogonal to primary path U, V subspaces. Minimizes initial reconstruction overlap.
residual_path_init._note: Residual starts small — grows during training as needed

gradient_estimation._comment: STE-v2: Adaptive Straight-Through Estimator for binary weight gradients
gradient_estimation.description: Modified STE with per-layer dynamic clipping. Standard STE clips at ±1 uniformly. STE-v2 clips at ±σ_layer where σ_layer is the running std of activations — prevents gradient vanishing in deep binary networks.
gradient_estimation._ste_scale_note: Binary factors get 1% of gradient magnitude — scales get full gradient. Binary topology evolves slowly.

training._comment: Full from-scratch QAT training recipe — 2T tokens, progressive binarization
training._batch_note: 4M token batch = ~512 seqs × 8192 tokens — standard for 70B-scale training

progressive_binarization_schedule._comment: PBS: Anneal precision from BF16 → binary over first 5% of training (100B tokens)
progressive_binarization_schedule.description: Start training in full BF16. Progressively reduce weight precision in stages. Binary weights are introduced layer-by-layer from top to bottom. This stabilizes gradient flow during the critical early phase.

phases.name: Full Precision Warm-up
phases._note: Standard BF16 pre-training. Build stable representations before quantization.

phases.name: High-Bit Quantization Annealing
phases._note: Introduce 4-bit weights starting from bottom layers (closer to input = more sensitive → quantize later)

phases.name: Sub-1-bit Transition
phases._note: Switch to latent 2-bit representation. Introduce U, V factors. All scale factors now active.

phases.name: Binary Convergence
phases._note: Full binarization of U, V. Primary path only. No residual yet.

phases.name: Residual Activation
phases._note: Activate residual path. Full 0.1 BPW dual-path training.

phases.name: Main Training Run
phases._note: Standard LLM training at 0.1 BPW. 1.8T tokens of learning.

self_distillation._comment: EMA teacher provides stable soft targets — compensates for lack of pre-trained teacher
self_distillation.description: Maintain EMA copy of scale factors in BF16. Teacher logits are computed using EMA scales with same binary weights. Student (current model) distills from EMA teacher.
self_distillation._teacher_cost: ~0% extra memory — EMA only updates scale vectors, not binary matrices

parameter_groups._comment: Differential LR — scales learn fast, binary latent factors learn slow

scale_factors._note: Scales are the primary learning medium at 0.1 BPW

binary_factors_U_V._note: Binary factors update via STE. Very small effective LR. Topology changes slowly.

router_weights._note: Router needs higher LR for fast expert assignment convergence

training_precision._optimizer_note: Adam states in FP32 even for scale factors — prevents optimizer state underflow

data._comment: 2T token curriculum. Web → code → math → instruction fine-tune.

training_mixture._comment: Token mixture across training phases — shifts from general → specialized

infrastructure._comment: Designed for A100/H100 cluster. Scales from 64 GPUs (fast iteration) to 1024 GPUs (full run).

parallelism._context_parallel_note: Context parallelism splits 128K sequence across 4 GPUs for long-context training
parallelism._expert_parallel_note: MoE experts sharded across 2 GPUs — 4 experts per GPU
parallelism._zero_note: ZeRO-3 for optimizer states — critical since FP32 Adam states are largest memory consumer

recommended_cluster_sizes.minimum_viable: 64× H100 (8 nodes) — fits pipeline with TP=8, PP=4, DP=2
recommended_cluster_sizes.recommended: 256× H100 (32 nodes) — DP=8 for good throughput

binary_gemv_cuda.description: XNOR + popcount based GEMV for binary U, V matrix-vector products
binary_gemv_cuda.implementation: custom CUDA with Tensor Core fallback

fused_scale_apply.description: Fuse row/col/latent scale application into single kernel after binary GEMV

packed_binary_format.description: Pack 8 binary values per byte (int8), apply sign via bitwise ops

inference._comment: Serving configuration — target: 200+ tok/s on single A100, 50+ tok/s on RTX 3090
inference._dtype_note: Binary weights auto-detected and dispatched to binary GEMV kernel

kv_cache._memory_note: At 128K context, KV cache = 80 layers × 2 paths × 205 × 131072 × 2 bytes ≈ 8.8 GB. Standard attention would be 80 × 2 × 8192 × 131072 × 2 bytes ≈ 350 GB.

speculative_decoding._draft_note: Train companion 1B draft model at same 0.1 BPW — draft fits in ~30 MB. 3-5 token speculation.

throughput_estimates._rtx3090_note: RTX 3090 (24 GB VRAM) fits model comfortably. No CPU offload needed.

cloud_server._note: Full 70B-nominal model on single GPU — first time in history for this parameter class

edge_device.target: Apple M3 Max or Snapdragon X Elite

evaluation._comment: Benchmark suite targeting top-1 performance across all major evals

perplexity_targets._baseline_comparison: Llama2-70B FP16 achieves WikiText2 PPL ~3.3. We target PPL within 2× at 31× compression.

quantization_quality_gate._gate_note: If PPL degrades >2.5× or reasoning drops >15% vs BF16, trigger automatic PBS phase rollback

sft_phase._note: Run SFT after main pre-training. Keep binary weights frozen, only update scales + embeddings.

rlhf_phase._dpo_note: DPO preferred over PPO for binary models — PPO reward modeling is unstable at 0.1 BPW

risks_and_mitigations._comment: Честные предупреждения — сеньор архитектор не скрывает риски

risk_1_training_instability.description: Binary weights cause massive gradient variance, especially in early phases. Model can diverge.
risk_1_training_instability.mitigation: Progressive Binarization Schedule (PBS). Never go directly to binary. Warm up 10B tokens first.
risk_1_training_instability.fallback: If loss spikes >2σ, revert to previous PBS phase and reduce LR by 50%.

risk_2_moe_collapse.description: MoE routing can collapse to 1-2 experts, eliminating capacity benefit.
risk_2_moe_collapse.mitigation: Z-loss + load balancing loss. Monitor expert utilization every 1B tokens.
risk_2_moe_collapse.threshold: Alert if any expert handles >40% of tokens for >100M token window.

risk_3_embedding_bottleneck.description: BF16 embeddings (2.1 GB) dominate model size for edge deployment.
risk_3_embedding_bottleneck.mitigation: Optional 4-bit embedding quantization post-training reduces to ~530 MB.

risk_4_capability_gap.description: At 0.1 BPW, fundamental information capacity is limited regardless of architecture.
risk_4_capability_gap.realistic_expectation: Competitive with 7B-13B dense models on most benchmarks despite 70B nominal size. Top-1 claim is within binary/sub-1-bit category, not vs FP16 70B class.
risk_4_capability_gap.mitigation: Scale rank (r) if resources allow — going to r=410 doubles BPW to ~0.2 but dramatically improves quality.

risk_5_ste_gradient_noise.description: STE introduces biased gradient estimates. Scale factors may overfit to compensate.
risk_5_ste_gradient_noise.mitigation: Adaptive STE-v2 with per-layer dynamic clipping. Regularize scale factors separately.

risk_6_long_context_degradation.description: Latent KV cache (dim=205) compresses attention pattern information.
risk_6_long_context_degradation.mitigation: YaRN position encoding + sliding window attention hybrid. Evaluated at 4K, 32K, 128K separately.

reproducibility._deterministic_note: Full determinism disabled for performance. Use fixed seed for reproducible sampling only.
reproducibility._anomaly_note: Auto-detect and skip corrupted batches (NaN loss spikes from GPU errors)

mixture_of_ranks.description: Assign different ranks (r) per layer based on sensitivity analysis. Critical early layers get higher rank (r=400), later layers lower (r=100). Can achieve same BPW with better quality.

dynamic_rank_pruning.description: Prune latent dimensions with consistently near-zero latent scale (|l_i| < threshold). Reduces effective rank during inference.

multimodal_extension.description: Add vision encoder (BF16 ViT) with cross-attention projection quantized at 0.1 BPW.

quantum_classical_hybrid.description: Replace MoE routing with QUBO-based token-to-expert assignment (QPanda3 / pyqpanda-algorithm). QPCA pre-processes token embeddings to extract low-dim routing features.
quantum_classical_hybrid._personal_note: Compatible with existing QMAXS pipeline from Resonance agent
